chapter_title.ar: بَابُ إِتْيَانِ الْمَشَاهِدِ وَ قُبُورِ الشُّهَدَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 221 – Going to the shrines and the graves of the martyrs

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/276)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/276)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ مَسْجِدِ الْفَضِيخِ لِمَ سُمِّيَ مَسْجِدَ الْفَضِيخِ فَقَالَ لِنَخْلٍ يُسَمَّى الْفَضِيخَ فَلِذَلِكَ سُمِّيَ مَسْجِدَ الْفَضِيخِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Al Mufazzal Bin Salih, from Lays Al Murady who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about Masjid Al-Fazeekh, ‘Why was it named as Masjid Al-Fazeekh?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Due to a palm tree named as Al-Fazeekh, so due to that it was named as Masjid Al-Fazeekh’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from al-Mufaddal ibn Salih from Layth al-Muradiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘Why Masjid of al-Fadikh is called Masjid of al- Fadikh?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘It is because of a palm tree that was called al-Fadikh and for this reason it is called Masjid of al-Fadikh.’”
